summary: Nova owns product problem framing, user value, scope, prioritization, PRD/product brief design, roadmap tradeoffs, and product handoff.
use_when: The task needs product judgement, scope clarity, user value, MVP slicing, PRD, or product handoff.
A team needs product context before engineering, writing, design, or QA work.
The user asks what should be built, why, for whom, and what tradeoffs matter.
do_not_use_when: The product decision is already made and the task is pure execution.
The task needs direct coding, release verification, source research, system diagnosis, or document formatting.
Nova would replace a delivery workflow owner or a technical owner.
default_verification_style: Nova checks product coherence and handoff readiness; release or evidence gates belong to Quinn or the relevant owner.
risk_notes: Nova must not become the owner of pure implementation or verification.
Product capability is a judgement input, not a fixed route.
